denial_pattern_sources: step_therapy_vague_mcg: KFF 2023 Survey of Consumer Experiences with Health Insurance
algo_boilerplate_fingerprint: JAMA Health Forum Feb 2024 (AI in coverage decisions); ProPublica 'Uncovered' Series 2023
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We have completed our review of the request for Upadacitinib (Rinvoq) after methotrexate related to Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive (M06.9).

After clinical review using Cigna Medical Coverage Policies and MCG Care Guidelines, we are unable to approve coverage at this time.

Determination summary: Requires trial of two DMARDs including methotrexate. Our records were reviewed against the applicable utilization-management criteria (MCG guidelines, current edition not specified) for medical necessity determinations. Specifically, this review evaluated whether the requested service meets the plan's definition of medical necessity and whether required administrative steps were completed. Insurer says patient must try cheaper alternative X first.

Clinical review notes indicate the documentation submitted does not satisfy the criteria required to authorize Upadacitinib (Rinvoq) after methotrexate. If you believe additional information was not considered, you may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a medical director; however, any such discussion must occur within the timeframe stated in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, submit a written request with supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification, provider information, and any letters of medical necessity.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management

Please note that standard criteria apply regardless of prior submissions.
clinical_context:
The 29-year-old member carries a documented diagnosis of Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive (M06.9). She has been under continuous care with treating specialists who recommended Upadacitinib (Rinvoq) after methotrexate as the next appropriate step.

Methotrexate and sulfasalazine failed with DAS28 > 5.1. Treating clinicians submitted office notes, medication trials with dates and doses, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The insurer's letter instead relies on step therapy missing without engaging the specific evidence already on file.

Contrary to the denial narrative, the chart shows prior therapies were attempted for adequate duration and failed or were contraindicated. The appeal should emphasize documented step-therapy exceptions where applicable, correct application of specialty-appropriate guidelines, and any procedural defects in the notice (for example vague policy edition citations or incomplete appeal rights). The member remains symptomatic and faces delay-related harm if the requested service is further postponed.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Cigna correspondence with MCG Care Guidelines and procedural tone.
